title: luzula campestris subsp. congesta
intro: Luzula campestris subsp. congesta, also known as congested woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is a perennial herb that is often found in mountainous and subalpine regions, typically growing in acidic soils and rocky areas. The congested woodrush has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
